text: I assume we’ll have a ton of possession and face a low block. We have to be clinical in front of goal. The surprise results thus far had the more favored team have chance after chance, but didn’t have the right mix of creativity, patience and a willingness to get into the box and sacrifice for a goal. I’m excited to see who will be that player for us. Wes is someone I think will be incredibly important tomorrow for multiple reason, but I think his ability to win the ball in the air will be huge. If Puli plays, his greatest asset is moving off the ball and predicting the danger areas. Defensively we have to be smart and understand Aus wants us to have 8 players in our attacking third, so they can play kickball. Dest and Jedi will be incredibly important tomorrow. They have to know their role offensively and defensively. If not it will expose Ream due to his lack of pace and Freeman’s lack of experience. And for the love of goodness, Tyler, please stay out of the books. Pulisic situation is tricky. If he’s healthy obviously everyone wants him to play. But let’s just think about this deeper cause who knows how his health is right now. Our team understands Pulisic’s importance of course, but do they believe they can win it all or make a deep run if Puli isn’t at his best? I don’t think very many do. Yes, I know it’s only Aus, but what if he doesn’t play and we play just as well as we did in our first match?! Now that belief gets stronger, the pressure begins to loosen on Puli, and our team understands, we don’t need Puli to be Messi, we just need him to do his job. The mental side is just as important as the physical and luck or fortune side. This could set our team well ahead of so many others if you handle this right. Disclaimer: by no means in any world am I saying don’t play a healthy Puli so we can get better mentally. Call me what you want, but there is something special about this TEAM. And I put team in all caps because I truly feel we are one of the better teams right now. Are we in the same stadium when it comes to Spain, France, or even England on a talent level, of course not. However, when it comes to togetherness, believing that the person next to you will do their job instead of trying to be a super hero, and the heart to persevere, we not only are in the same stadium those teams, we’re right there. Yes it was one match, but this team is no stranger to struggle or defeat. In fact, we had a ton of that in the lead up to this WC and I think that’s only made us stronger. Win or lose tomorrow and no matter how deep we go in this tournament, I’m grateful to be alive and witness the greatest WC in my young 35 years of life. I’m proud to be an American. Let’s GO USA!!!
label: tactical_analysis

text: You'll have a full hour at a WELCOME dinner? I just got married. Welcome dinner is not a big deal. We had people showing up late, leaving early to go fuck, bar hopping, etc. This is the goddamn World Cup, on home soil, in Seattle no less.
